page: Microsoft Azure E48d_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EDv5 server family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The configuration features 48 virtual CPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. It includes a substantial memory allocation of 384.0 GB RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio. Additionally, the instance is equipped with 1843 GB of local NVMe storage, while containing no hardware GPU accelerators.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong performance in memory-intensive and database operations. The server achieved a PassMark CPU score of 50821.1 and a PassMark memory score of 2851.15, both placing in the top 25% tier. Database operations reached 23256.75 on PassMark, reflecting high efficiency for transactional workloads. In terms of memory bandwidth, the system recorded 2504.1 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 150.89 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. For computational tasks, the multi-core stress-ng CPU performance registered at 46600.84, while Geekbench image processing demonstrated strong capabilities with a score of 15066.0.

The hardware profile presents specific performance tradeoffs. While the server excels in memory capacity and database operations, its performance on large-scale LLM text generation is limited, with the 70B model achieving only 2.69 tokens/sec, placing it in the bottom 25% tier. This indicates that while the CPU can handle smaller model inference, it is not optimized for massive LLM workloads. Qualitatively, the inclusion of a large 1843 GB local disk alongside a high memory-to-vCPU ratio offers strong resource density and cost efficiency for memory-heavy applications without requiring external storage overhead. The E48d_v5 is highly suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data analytics, and enterprise applications requiring high memory capacity and fast local scratch space.
description: Microsoft Azure E48d_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDv5 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores, 384.0 GB of RAM, and 1843 GB of local storage. Benchmark results highlight strong performance, particularly in PassMark database operations and memory tests, which rank in the top 25% tier. The instance also demonstrates high memory bandwidth, reaching 2504.1 GB/sec for cached small-block reads. However, it exhibits lower performance in large-scale LLM text generation, making it less suitable for heavy AI workloads. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and integrated local storage, this server is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, including relational databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48d_v5 is a memory-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 1843 GB local storage, delivering strong database and memory performance for enterprise workloads.

tagline: A high-capacity memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and integrated local storage for demanding database and analytical workloads.
